description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest Docker image with a docker:dind service and sets the DOCKER_DRIVER variable to overlay. Define a before_script that runs: apk add --no-cache py-pip; pip install docker-compose; docker-compose --version; export IMG_TAG=`if [ "$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME" == "master" ]; then echo "latest"; else echo $CI_BUILD_REF_NAME | sed -e 's/[\/]/-/g' | sed -e 's/[\#]//g'; fi`; export DOCKER_IMAGE_PROD=$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$IMG_TAG; docker login -u "gitlab-ci-token" -p "$CI_BUILD_TOKEN" $CI_REGISTRY; export CI_BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"); export CI_BUILDER=gitlab-ci; cp .env.example .env. Add a single build stage with a build-verify job that runs the following script commands: docker-compose run --rm dev yarn; docker-compose run --rm dev yarn run lint; docker-compose run --rm dev ng test --browsers Chrome_no_sandbox -w false; docker-compose run --rm dev yarn run e2e; docker-compose run --rm dev ng build --prod; docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml build prod; docker push $DOCKER_IMAGE_PROD. The build-verify job runs in the build stage and does not define any artifacts, caches, or conditional rules.
